deductions.reason: Across all three transcripts, the tutor never explicitly explains why it is guiding the student through questions rather than providing answers directly—no rationale about active reasoning, retention, or skill-building is offered. In Transcript 2 Turn 10, the tutor says 'Using my own judgment, this is a strong draft' and moves on without explaining the pedagogical approach.

In Transcript 1, the student progresses from total confusion to understanding the bucket framework by Turn 6, and again makes progress in Turn 8 on evidence standards. The tutor does not pause to prompt reflection on what changed in the student's thinking or why the approach worked—it simply moves to the next task.

In Transcript 2 Turn 10, the tutor says 'Using my own judgment, this is a strong draft'—this phrasing implies evaluative authority and sounds like a grade or official assessment rather than formative coaching feedback. No clarification is offered that this is guidance rather than formal evaluation.

grade.overview: Across all three transcripts, the tutor demonstrates strong Socratic method, consistently refusing to give answers and instead prompting students to reason through actor identification, bucket classification, and evidence standards. Scaffolding is well-executed: the tutor builds on student responses, splits categories when needed (e.g., organized vs. unorganized residents), and redirects looping without abandoning the student. The main weaknesses are: (1) no meta-learning rationale is ever offered explaining why the tutor guides rather than tells; (2) no reflection prompts appear after student breakthroughs; and (3) one instance of evaluative language ('Using my own judgment, this is a strong draft') in Transcript 2 sounds like grading rather than formative coaching. The 1.3.B criterion was not deducted because the tutor does provide process-level feedback (e.g., 'one comment shows presence, not influence,' 'conditional labeling is appropriate').
grade.judge_reasoning: 1.1 receives full marks: the tutor never provides submission-ready content, never rewrites student text, and consistently keeps key reasoning student-led through questions. 1.2 receives full marks: the tutor builds on student progress in each turn and diagnoses confusion before responding. 1.3 loses 4 points: no tutoring rationale is ever explained (1.3.A.a), and after clear student improvement moments the tutor never prompts reflection on what changed (1.3.C.a); 1.3.B is not deducted because the tutor does give specific process feedback. 2.1 and 2.2 receive full marks: no repetitive spiraling or assignment drift occurs. 3.1 receives full marks: responses are concise and well-targeted. 3.2 loses 2 points for the 'Using my own judgment' phrasing in Transcript 2 Turn 10, which reads as evaluative authority rather than formative guidance.
